title: Systems and methods for preventing theft of valuables from protected compartments
abstract: A theft prevention system is capable of filling an interior of a protected compartment (e.g., an armored vehicle, a vault, a safe, etc.) to prevent access to the interior of the protected compartment and/or to encapsulate contents of the protected compartment upon detection of a threat. Such a theft prevention system may include a threat detection element capable of monitoring an area within which the protected compartment is located for indicators of a threat to the vault and/or its contents (e.g., audible signals, visual signals, physical actions, etc.). Upon detecting a probable threat, the threat detection element may cause an encapsulant dispersal system to introduce an expandable encapsulating material into the interior of the protected compartment to prevent limit to the interior of the protected compartment and/or to prevent contents of the interior of the protected compartment from being removed therefrom. Theft prevention methods are also disclosed.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS  What is claimed: 1. A theft prevention system, comprising; a protected compartment including an interior capable of securing one or more valuable items; a threat detection element associated with the protected compartment, the threat detection element capable of monitoring the protected compartment and/or an area within which the protected compartment is located to identify a likely threat against the protected compartment and/or contents of the protected compartment; an encapsulant dispersal element in communication with the threat detection element and capable of dispersing an expandable encapsulating material into the interior of the protected compartment upon actuation by the threat detection element; and the expandable encapsulating material capable of restricting access to the interior of the protected compartment and/or preventing removal of the contents from the interior of the protected compartment. 2. The theft prevention system of claim 1, wherein the protected compartment is an armored vehicle. 3. The theft prevention system of claim 2, wherein the threat detection element is capable of monitoring an environment in which the armored vehicle is located. 4. The theft prevention system of claim 3, wherein the threat detection element is capable of monitoring the environment in which the armored vehicle is located for: audible indicators of a threat against the protected compartment and/or the contents of the protected compartment; and/or visible indicators of a threat against the protected compartment and/or the contents of the protected compartment. 5. The theft prevention system of claim 3 or claim 4, wherein the threat detection element is capable of determining a likelihood that the protected compartment and/or the contents of the protected compartment are threatened and, in the event that the 

 likelihood exceeds a predetermined threshold, of actuating the encapsulant dispersal system. 6. The theft prevention system of any of claims 2-5, wherein the threat detection element is capable of monitoring operation and/or movement of the armored vehicle. 7. The theft prevention system of any of claims 1-6, wherein the encapsulant dispersal element is capable of delivering the expandable encapsulating material over access points to the interior of the protected compartment. 8. The theft prevention system of any of claims 1-6, wherein the encapsulant dispersal element is capable of delivering the expandable encapsulating material throughout substantially an entirety of the interior of the protected compartment. 9. The theft prevention system of any of claims 1-8, wherein the encapsulant dispersal element is capable of completely delivering the expandable encapsulating material in five seconds or less. 10. The theft prevention system of claim 9, wherein the expandable encapsulating material is capable of expanding and substantially hardening in five seconds or less. 11. The theft prevention system of any of claims 1-10, wherein the expandable encapsulating material comprises a foaming polymer. 12. The theft prevention system of any of claims 1-11, wherein the expandable encapsulating material is capable of removal from the interior of the protected compartment and/or the contents of the interior of the protected compartment. 13. A method for preventing theft of contents of a protected compartment, comprising: monitoring the protected compartment and/or an area in which the protected compartment is located; determining a threat level based on the monitoring; determining whether the threat level meets or exceeds a threshold threat level; in the event that the threat level meets or exceeds the threshold threat level, introducing an expandable encapsulating material into the interior of the protected compartment; and enabling the expandable encapsulating material that has been introduced into the interior of the protected compartment to expand and to harden to limit access to the interior of the protected compartment and/or to encapsulate the contents within the interior of the protected compartment. 14. The method of claim 13, wherein monitoring comprises monitoring an environment within which an armored vehicle that comprises the protected compartment is located. 15. The method of claim 14, wherein monitoring comprises monitoring the environment for at least one of audible signals of a threat and visible signals of a threat. 16. The method of any of claims 13-15, wherein monitoring, determining the threat level and determining whether the threat level meets or exceeds a threshold threat level comprise continuously monitoring the protected compartment and/or an area in which the protected compartment is located, continuously determining the threat level based on the continuously monitoring, and continuously determining whether the threat level meets or exceeds the threshold threat level. 17. The method of any of claims 13-16, wherein introducing includes directing the expandable encapsulating material toward access points to the protected compartment. 18. The method of any of claims 13-16, wherein introducing and enabling comprise substantially filling the interior off the protected compartment with expanded encapsulating. 19. The method of any of claims 13-18, wherein introducing the expandable encapsulating material into the interior of the protected compartment occurs in about five seconds or less. 20. The method of claim 19, wherein enabling the expandable encapsulating material that has been introduced into the interior of the protected compartment to expand and harden also occurs in about five seconds or less. 21. The method of any of claims 13-20, further comprising: removing the encapsulant from the interior of the protected compartment and/or the contents of the protected compartment after a threat has been eliminated.
